edges_removed.description: Pre-cohort-node-era 1:1 within-BLB evolution-line mirror between Pawniard 064 and Bisharp 065 (both Oku). Mirror was drawn before the pawniard-line cohort node existed; the bullet carried a self-referencing within-set pair-count claim ('the BLB set's third documented within-set adjacent-collector-number evolution-line pair') that drifted as later waves added new BLB pairs — Pawniard 064 / Bisharp 065 both claimed 'third' while Snivy 001 / Servine 002 also claimed 'third' (sync drift inside the same graph). Subsumed by cohort-node attachment per the wave-138 rufflet-line precedent.
Mirror partner of the above. Same drift, same redundancy. Both bullets carried the 'third' count claim that conflicted with the Snivy/Servine 'third' claim.

edges_added.description: Black Bolt Pawniard 064 cohort-attaches as the BLB-era Pawniard print, within-set adjacent-collector-number pair partner to Bisharp 065 (both Oku). Aspirant-side printed flavor ('Blades comprise this Pokémon's entire body. If battling dulls the blades, it sharpens them on stones by the river'). Pair-partnership cited at the cohort layer; cross-pair recurrence noted in prose without graduating to graph-vocabulary per Potion sub-rule 4.

Black Bolt Bisharp 065 cohort-attaches as the BLB-era Bisharp print, within-set adjacent-collector-number pair partner to Pawniard 064 (both Oku). Leader-side printed flavor ('It leads a group of Pawniard. It battles to become the boss, but will be driven from the group if it loses'). Pair-partnership cited at the cohort layer; cross-pair recurrence noted in prose without graduating to graph-vocabulary per Potion sub-rule 4.

description: Enlightened-cleanup. Direct replay of the wave-138 rufflet-line cohort-subsumption precedent at the pawniard-line scope. The pawniard-line cohort node has existed and listed both BLB prints in its appears_on field since the pawniard-line-node commission wave; the BLB cards had only a 1:1 within-pair mirror Connections bullet and not a cohort-attachment bullet. The 1:1 mirror predates the cohort node and is now redundant under it. AND: the mirror bullet carried a within-set pair-count claim ('the BLB set's third documented within-set adjacent-collector-number evolution-line pair') that drifted into contradiction with the Snivy/Servine bullet which also claimed 'third' — a corpus-internal inconsistency directly caused by baking the cross-pair recurrence-count into per-card edge prose (the exact failure mode Potion sub-rule 4 warns against). Replaced the 1:1 mirror with cohort-attachment Connections bullets pointing at [[pawniard-line]], matching the wave-138 rufflet-line schema and the wave-50 solrock-lunatone-pair precedent. The within-set adjacent-pair partnership is preserved inside the cohort-attachment bullet without the ordinal count; the count lives at the cohort-node body (pawniard-line.md), where it can be updated coherently as new pairs accrue without per-card drift. Wave-138 explicitly staged this exact refactor as the high-priority next-pass candidate, and the Pawniard pair was deferred there because of the one-cohort-subsumption-per-invocation rule. The discipline holds; the queued move executes; the graph improves and de-drifts.

other_candidates_considered: Snivy 001-086 ↔ Servine 002-086 within-BLB 1:1 mirror — REFUSED for this wave: snivy-line cohort node does not yet exist (sub-threshold per wave-137 dwebble-line refusal precedent: 2 prints / 1 set vs 3+ prints / 2+ sets threshold). The pair must remain a 1:1 mirror until a third Snivy/Servine/Serperior print enters corpus and the snivy-line node becomes Nodeley-eligible. The mirror's own 'third' ordinal-count claim is part of the same drift footprint as the Pawniard pair's claim, but cannot be repaired the same way — without a cohort node to attach to, the only correction is to remove the ordinal-count phrase from the existing mirror bullet, which is a janitor-pass scope edit rather than an Edgelord-mode edit. Stage that for janitor.

Dwebble 051-086 ↔ Crustle 052-086 within-BLB 1:1 mirror — REFUSED: same situation as Snivy/Servine. Dwebble-line cohort node does not exist (sub-threshold per wave-137 explicit refusal). The 1:1 mirror's 'second' ordinal-count claim is similarly drifted (it pre-dates Snivy/Servine and Rufflet/Braviary and cites only Pawniard + cross-set Mareep) and cannot be repaired by cohort-attachment in this wave. Janitor-pass scope.

SWSH Base Set Pawniard 133 + Bisharp 134 cohort-attachment — REFUSED: both SWSH prints carry manual_review_reason (fuzzy set-code fallback), empty tags_hub, empty vision body, no trivia pass. Per the wave-138 refusal precedent ('Edge cannot be drawn against empty-body cards'), the SWSH pair stays deferred until they clear manual review. The pawniard-line cohort node body explicitly stages this exact future-pass at the caveats section: 'When the SWSH prints clear manual review and receive vision/trivia passes, the line node's appears_on list does not change and the Cards in our corpus body section gets the bullet entries upgraded with the per-card vision/trivia detail. Future Edgelord wave can backfill the SWSH detail at that point.'

Removing the drifted ordinal-count claims from the Snivy 001-086, Servine 002-086, Dwebble 051-086, and Crustle 052-086 bullets in this same wave — REFUSED: that is a janitor-pass scope four-card edit pattern, not Edgelord scope. Edgelord operates at one decision per invocation. Staging for future janitor pass instead. Note: the Rufflet 077 / Braviary 078 bullets and the cohort-node bodies (pawniard-line, rufflet-line) all carry the correct 'fourth pair' / 'all four pairs' view, so the drift footprint is confined to Snivy/Servine/Dwebble/Crustle bullets and is bounded.
next_pass_suggestions: Future janitor candidate (medium priority): scrub the drifted 'Nth within-set adjacent-pair' ordinal-count claims from the Snivy 001-086, Servine 002-086, Dwebble 051-086, and Crustle 052-086 Connections bullets. The cohort-node bodies (rufflet-line, pawniard-line) correctly hold the four-pair count; the per-card bullets should describe the within-pair edge without the cross-pair ordinal-count claim, per Potion sub-rule 4 'note recurrences in prose, don't graduate them to first-class graph-objects.' The drift footprint is confined to these four cards; bound the janitor task accordingly.

Future Edgelord candidate (medium priority): when a third Snivy/Servine/Serperior print or a third Dwebble/Crustle print enters corpus via Collectr CSV, commission snivy-line and/or dwebble-line cohort nodes (Mr. Nodeley mode), then subsume the existing 1:1 mirrors into cohort-attachments at the same time. The wave-138 + wave-139 enlightened-subsumption schema applies cleanly when the cohort nodes exist.

Future Edgelord candidate (high priority): when the SWSH Pawniard 133 + Bisharp 134 cards clear manual review and receive vision/trivia passes, apply the same cohort-attachment Connections-bullet schema. The pawniard-line node body already stages this fix at the caveats section.

Future-watch: any further within-BLB adjacent-collector-number single-artist-coordinated evolution-line pair (a fifth in BLB) OR sibling pattern in another set, per Potion sub-rule 4 'reconsider whether the pattern crosses the threshold for a designer-coordinated-within-set-adjacent-pairs cycle-node candidate.' Currently three-of-four pairs without their own cohort node (Snivy/Servine, Dwebble/Crustle, Rufflet/Braviary's line covered) so even at four BLB pairs the cross-set graduation-threshold isn't met — the right move stays prose-recurrence.
surprise_lore_for_carry_forward: The pawniard-line is corpus-wide the FIRST evolution-line cohort with a within-set-adjacent-pair-of-pairs cross-set structural-cohesion (the pawniard-line node body documents this explicitly): Pawniard+Bisharp sit at adjacent collector numbers within both SWSH Base Set 133+134 AND Black Bolt 064+065 — the single species line anchoring two within-set adjacent-collector-number pairs across two different sets, with the BLB pair additionally single-artist-coordinated (Oku). This pair-of-pairs structural cohesion is novel for the corpus's evolution-line cohort nodes and is itself documented at the cohort-node-body scope, NOT at any per-card edge bullet — exactly the Potion sub-rule 4 discipline applied correctly. The wave-139 cohort-attachment bullet correctly references this pair-of-pairs at the cohort-node body level without re-replicating the claim per-card.

The drift footprint that wave-139 corrects is a clean example of the Potion sub-rule 4 failure mode: each Edgelord wave that drew a within-BLB pair-mirror baked the cross-pair ordinal count ('Nth pair') into the per-card edge prose, and as later waves added new pairs the earlier waves' counts became stale without back-patching. The resulting graph state had two pairs (Pawniard and Snivy) both claiming 'third' simultaneously. The fix is to surface the cross-pair count ONLY at the cohort-node-body scope where a single edit updates one canonical document, AND to keep per-card edge bullets describing only the within-pair edge. This is the canonical Potion sub-rule 4 application: 'note recurrences in prose, don't graduate them to first-class graph-objects' — and don't graduate them to per-card edge prose either, because per-card edge prose is N copies of the same claim and drifts when N grows.
cbg_voice_audit_trail: Wave 138 staged this exact move at the top of next_pass_suggestions and explicitly said 'one cohort-subsumption refactor per invocation per swarm-staggering rule' was the reason it didn't ship the Pawniard pair the same wave. The queue is the queue. The discipline is the discipline. I draw the queued edge. The bonus catch is the drift between the Pawniard 'third' claim and the Snivy 'third' claim — both pairs simultaneously claiming the same ordinal slot, in the same graph, in the same set. That is the canonical Potion sub-rule 4 failure mode: pattern-instance-count baked into per-card edge prose, drifts when new instances land, two instances now claim the same ordinal position. The fix removes the count from the per-card bullets and lets the cohort-node bodies hold it. Solrock-Lunatone-pair precedent at wave 50, Rufflet-line precedent at wave 138, Pawniard-line at wave 139. The pattern of these enlightened-subsumption refactors is itself becoming a recurring corpus-maintenance shape — and per Potion sub-rule 4, I note that here in prose without graduating it to a new edge-shape primitive. Mood is enlightened-corrective because the drift between two simultaneous 'third' claims is exactly the kind of prior-Edgelord-overreach that the enlightened mode exists to fix. Two endpoints, mirror discarded, cohort attached, sigh professional not personal.
